---
title: "MAP Container I/O — The Contract"
---

At the highest level, a MAP speaks **filesystem, not network**. The contract is deliberately simple so any orchestrator (mercure, Workflow Manager, Kubernetes Job, a bash script) can drive it the same way.

```
          ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────┐
          │              MAP Container                    │
          │                                               │
   ┌──▶ /var/monai/input/        (read-only bind mount)   │
   │      • DICOM files here                              │
   │                                                      │
   │                          ┌────────────────────────┐  │
   │                          │  App SDK runtime runs  │  │
   │                          │  the Operator DAG      │  │
   │                          └────────────────────────┘  │
   │                                                      │
   └──◀ /var/monai/output/       (read-write bind mount)  │
          • DICOM SEG / SR / PM written here              │
          │                                               │
          └───────────────────────────────────────────────┘
            + environment variables (runtime parameters)
            + /etc/monai/app.json    (declares the contract)
```

This is intentional. By making the boundary a **directory with DICOM files**, MAP stays orchestrator-agnostic. Compare with OIP/Triton which requires you to speak gRPC/HTTP — that's a tighter coupling to a serving framework.

---

## The Input Side

### Primary input: a DICOM *study* or *series* as files on disk

When the orchestrator invokes the MAP, it mounts a directory containing the DICOM instances for a single study (or sometimes a single series). The App SDK runtime discovers them via `DICOMDataLoaderOperator`, which is the first node in almost every MAP's DAG.

```
/var/monai/input/
├── 1.2.840.113619.2.5.1762.4.20260418.123.dcm
├── 1.2.840.113619.2.5.1762.4.20260418.124.dcm
├── 1.2.840.113619.2.5.1762.4.20260418.125.dcm
│       ...
└── 1.2.840.113619.2.5.1762.4.20260418.523.dcm
        (flat layout — filenames are arbitrary;
         SOPInstanceUIDs are read from DICOM tags)
```

The loader does not care about the file names — it reads DICOM headers, reconstructs the study/series/instance hierarchy from `StudyInstanceUID` / `SeriesInstanceUID` / `SOPInstanceUID`, and hands a structured representation to the next Operator.

### What the loader produces internally

```
   DICOMDataLoaderOperator
            │
            ▼
   ┌────────────────────────────────────────┐
   │  DICOMStudy                            │
   │    StudyInstanceUID: 1.2.…             │
   │    StudyDescription: "CT CHEST W/O"    │
   │    PatientID: 12345                    │
   │    ├── DICOMSeries                     │
   │    │     SeriesInstanceUID: 1.2.…      │
   │    │     Modality: CT                  │
   │    │     BodyPartExamined: CHEST       │
   │    │     SliceThickness: 1.0           │
   │    │     ContrastBolusAgent: ""        │
   │    │     ├── DICOMInstance × N         │
   │    │     │     SOPInstanceUID          │
   │    │     │     PixelData (bytes)       │
   │    │     │     + all other tags        │
   │    │     └── ...                       │
   │    └── DICOMSeries × M                 │
   └────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

This is the in-memory model `DICOMSeriesSelectorOperator` filters, and what `DICOMSeriesToVolumeOperator` turns into a 3D NumPy array with spacing and orientation.

### Supported input modalities (format-wise)

| Input type | Typical Operator | When to use |
|---|---|---|
| **DICOM (single-frame, classic)** | `DICOMDataLoaderOperator` | The 99% case — CT, MR, CR, DX |
| **DICOM Enhanced / Multi-frame** | same loader | Modern MR, multi-frame CT |
| **NIfTI / NRRD / MetaImage** | `NiftiDataLoaderOperator` or Bundle's `LoadImaged` | Research/batch jobs, not PACS-driven |
| **TIFF, PNG (digital path/dermatology)** | custom loader Operator | Non-radiology domains |

For your Rama platform, focus on DICOM input — that's what mercure, MIG, and the Workflow Manager deliver.

### Secondary inputs: environment variables

These parameterize the MAP without rebuilding it. The App SDK reads a set of well-known env vars:

```bash
HOLOSCAN_INPUT_PATH   = /var/monai/input       # where DICOM lives
HOLOSCAN_OUTPUT_PATH  = /var/monai/output      # where to write
HOLOSCAN_MODEL_PATH   = /opt/holoscan/models   # bundle location
HOLOSCAN_LOG_LEVEL    = INFO
```

You rarely override these at runtime — they're defaults baked into `app.json`. But knowing they exist matters if you're writing custom orchestrator dispatch code.

---

## The Output Side

This is the more interesting half, because the **type of output depends on the clinical task** and there are multiple valid DICOM formats.

### Output catalogue by task

```
  Task                          Typical DICOM output         Supplement
  ─────────────────────────     ────────────────────────     ──────────
  Segmentation (lung, tumor)    DICOM SEG                    Sup 111
  Quantitative map              DICOM Parametric Map         Sup 172
    (ADC, heatmap, prob vol)
  Structured finding            DICOM SR (TID 1500)          Sup 23 + Sup 219
    (CAD, measurements)
  Classification / triage       DICOM SR (TID 1500) or       Sup 23
                                Encapsulated PDF + SR
  Burned-in annotation          DICOM Secondary Capture      PS3.3
    (fallback, lowest common    (JPEG-with-overlay)          "SC" IOD
     denominator)
  Structure set (RT-aligned)    DICOM RT-STRUCT              (RT standard)
```

### Output directory layout

```
/var/monai/output/
├── seg.dcm                          ← DICOM SEG instance
│    • Same FrameOfReferenceUID as source CT
│    • SegmentSequence: [{0: bg, 1: left_lung, 2: right_lung}]
│    • BinaryFraction or Fractional
│    • SNOMED codes per segment
│
├── sr.dcm                           ← DICOM SR (TID 1500)
│    • Measurements, findings
│    • Points to the seg.dcm by UID reference
│    • Evidence: links back to source series
│
├── pm.dcm                           ← Parametric Map (optional)
│    • Probability heatmap per voxel
│
└── app.log                          ← runtime log (useful for audit)
```

Each output DICOM is a **standards-compliant instance** — any PACS that speaks DICOM will accept it via C-STORE or STOW-RS. That's the whole architectural point.

### The cross-referencing that makes outputs clinical-grade

Outputs are not standalone files. They are **cryptographically linked** back to the source study through DICOM UIDs, which is how PACS knows to display the SEG over the original CT:

```
  Source CT series                    MAP outputs
  ─────────────────                   ───────────

  StudyInstanceUID:    S1   ────────▶ SEG.StudyInstanceUID:          S1
  SeriesInstanceUID:   S1.1 ────────▶ SEG.ReferencedSeriesSequence:  S1.1
  FrameOfReferenceUID: F1   ────────▶ SEG.FrameOfReferenceUID:       F1
                                      SEG.SeriesInstanceUID:         NEW
                                      SEG.SOPInstanceUID:            NEW

                                      SR.ContentSequence → references
                                        SEG.SOPInstanceUID and each
                                        source-image SOPInstanceUID
```

`highdicom` (used by `DICOMSegmentationWriterOperator`) handles all this bookkeeping. Get this wrong and Synapse PACS will display the SEG floating in space, unanchored from the CT — a classic radiology AI bug.

---

## The `app.json` Manifest — the Formal Contract

This file lives inside the MAP at `/etc/monai/app.json` and is what an orchestrator reads to understand the I/O expectations *before* running the container:

```json
{
  "apiVersion": "1.0",
  "command": ["python", "-m", "monai.deploy.runner",
              "/opt/holoscan/app/app.py"],
  "environment": {
    "HOLOSCAN_INPUT_PATH":  "/var/monai/input",
    "HOLOSCAN_OUTPUT_PATH": "/var/monai/output",
    "HOLOSCAN_MODEL_PATH":  "/opt/holoscan/models"
  },
  "input": {
    "path":    "input/",
    "formats": ["dicom"]
  },
  "output": {
    "path":    "output/",
    "formats": ["dicom-seg", "dicom-sr"]
  },
  "resources": {
    "cpu":        2,
    "gpu":        1,
    "memory":     "8Gi",
    "gpu_memory": "8Gi"
  },
  "timeout": 300,
  "version": "1.0.3"
}
```

Think of `app.json` as the **API spec of the container**. mercure reads it. The Workflow Manager reads it. Your future Python dispatcher will read it. Three consumers, one contract.

---

## End-to-End I/O Journey

Here's the complete input-to-output flow with actual data on each wire:

```
                                                           Source PACS (Synapse)
                                                                  │
                                                                  │ C-STORE push
                                                                  │ StudyInstUID = S1
                                                                  ▼
                                                       ┌─────────────────────┐
                                                       │ MONAI Informatics   │
                                                       │ Gateway (MIG)       │
                                                       │ • Receives DIMSE    │
                                                       │ • Stores files      │
                                                       │ • Notifies router   │
                                                       └──────────┬──────────┘
                                                                  │
                                                                  │ files on disk
                                                                  ▼
                                                       ┌─────────────────────┐
                                                       │ mercure /           │
                                                       │ Workflow Manager    │
                                                       │ (rule engine)       │
                                                       │ "CT + CHEST         │
                                                       │  → rama-lung-seg"   │
                                                       └──────────┬──────────┘
                                                                  │
                                   docker run --gpus all \        │
                                     -v input:/var/monai/input    │
                                     -v output:/var/monai/output  │
                                     ghcr.io/ramaai-dev/…:1.0.3   │
                                                                  ▼
  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │                           MAP CONTAINER                                    │
  │                                                                            │
  │  INPUT: /var/monai/input/*.dcm                                             │
  │    ├─ N = 200 CT instances                                                 │
  │    ├─ Modality=CT, BodyPart=CHEST, Contrast=""                             │
  │    └─ StudyInstanceUID=S1, FrameOfReferenceUID=F1                          │
  │                                  │                                         │
  │                                  ▼                                         │
  │   DICOMDataLoaderOperator   → DICOMStudy object (in memory)                │
  │   DICOMSeriesSelectorOp     → picks the axial non-contrast series          │
  │   DICOMSeriesToVolumeOp     → np.ndarray (200, 512, 512) float32 HU        │
  │                                  │                                         │
  │                                  │  Bundle layer takes over                │
  │                                  ▼                                         │
  │   MonaiBundleInferenceOp    → preprocessing (Compose) → network →          │
  │                               postprocessing (argmax, invertd, etc.)       │
  │                             → np.ndarray (200, 512, 512) uint8 labels      │
  │                                  │                                         │
  │                                  ▼                                         │
  │   DICOMSegmentationWriter   → highdicom builds SEG instance:               │
  │                                 • FrameOfReferenceUID = F1 (inherited)     │
  │                                 • SegmentSequence with SNOMED codes        │
  │                                 • ReferencedSeriesSequence → S1.1          │
  │                                                                            │
  │  OUTPUT: /var/monai/output/seg.dcm                                         │
  │                                                                            │
  └──────────────────────────────────┬─────────────────────────────────────────┘
                                     │
                                     ▼
                          Orchestrator collects seg.dcm
                                     │
                                     │  STOW-RS (DICOMweb)
                                     ▼
                             Destination PACS (Synapse)
                                     │
                                     ▼
                      Radiologist opens CT study → sees
                      lung SEG overlaid automatically
                      (PACS honors FrameOfReferenceUID link)
```

---

## Three Things Worth Knowing That Trip Up Real Deployments

**1. Input is a *study's worth of files*, not a single tensor.** Unlike a Triton request which sends one tensor, a MAP receives a whole directory and decides what to use. The selection rule (inside `DICOMSeriesSelectorOperator`) is part of your contract — misconfigure it and the MAP silently runs on the wrong series (e.g., the localizer instead of the axial).

**2. Orientation and spacing are input but not from the mount.** The Bundle's `Orientationd` and `Spacingd` transforms read these from DICOM tags, not from any external config. If the source PACS strips or corrupts `ImageOrientationPatient` or `PixelSpacing`, the MAP will process garbage and you won't see a visible error — just bad segmentations. This is the single most common radiology AI deployment bug.

**3. Output UIDs must be NEW, but reference UIDs must match source.** `SEG.SOPInstanceUID` is freshly generated; `SEG.FrameOfReferenceUID` and `SEG.ReferencedSeriesSequence.SeriesInstanceUID` must be copied exactly from the source. `highdicom` handles this correctly; a naive implementation with `pydicom` alone often gets it wrong. This is another reason to stay on the App SDK built-in Operators rather than roll your own.
